intro:
There is no information available on a plant called Chamaeriphes. However, I've found information on Chamaemelum nobile, also known as Chamaeriphes nobile or Roman chamomile, which appears to be a close match.

Chamaemelum nobile, or Roman chamomile, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and soothing properties, offering health benefits such as reducing anxiety, promoting relaxation, and alleviating insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Roman chamomile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, particularly apigenin and bisabolol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Roman chamomile include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, often used to promote sleep, calm the mind, and soothe digestive issues.
